novel_features.name: Price-Drop Watchlist
novel_features.description: Local watchlist of release ids; re-scans Blu-ray.com deals on demand and alerts when any watched disc hits its target price or a new historical low.
novel_features.rationale: Blu-ray.com has no native price-drop alerting. Cross-joining a local watchlist with the daily deals scan is only possible with a persistent local store.
novel_features.example: blu-ray-pp-cli watch add 9929 --target-price 14.99 && blu-ray-pp-cli watch check --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this whenever a user wants notifications about disc prices without polling third-party services. Pairs naturally with cron or a launchd job.

novel_features.name: Editions Side-By-Side
novel_features.description: Given a movie umbrella id, lists every disc edition (4K UHD, Blu-ray, Steelbook, Director's Cut, country variant) with release date, list price, current price, and Blu-ray.com community rating in a single view.
novel_features.rationale: Requires correlating the /main/<id>/ umbrella page with each per-format release detail page — a join no single Blu-ray.com page offers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a user is deciding which edition of a film to buy (4K vs Blu-ray, Criterion vs Arrow, region A vs region B). Surfaces the trade-off at a glance.

novel_features.name: UPC Import + Resolve
novel_features.description: Resolves a CSV of UPC codes (e.g. the comma-separated export Blu-ray.com itself produces) back to local release records, hydrating titles, formats, ratings, and current prices.
novel_features.rationale: Blu-ray.com's own export is UPC-only; nothing on the site round-trips UPCs back to structured release data. We close the gap with a one-shot local resolve.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this whenever a user is moving a collection between tools (Blu-ray.com to Trakt, CLZ, Letterboxd) or building a watch-list from a barcode scan.

novel_features.name: Catalog Drift
novel_features.description: Diffs today's sitemap against the last sync and surfaces 'new in catalog this week', 'removed', and 'metadata changed' so collectors don't miss announcements.
novel_features.rationale: Requires snapshotting the sitemap locally and diffing it longitudinally; no API call gives you 'what changed' directly.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to catch up after being away from the site for a week, or to build a weekly digest of what dropped, what got delayed, and what got pulled.

novel_features.name: Price History + Spark Plot
novel_features.description: Shows per-retailer price history for a release id, captured automatically by watch check + deals --record. Optional inline ASCII spark plot.
novel_features.rationale: Longitudinal price data persisted locally across runs — only possible with a local SQLite store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to know whether the current 'deal' is actually historically low, or just a small dip. Distinguishes real bargains from clickbait.

narrative.headline: The disc-collector's CLI for Blu-ray.com — offline catalog, live deals, and a price-drop watchlist with zero account required.
narrative.value_prop: Sync the public Blu-ray.com sitemap into a local SQLite + FTS5 index and search ~400,000 releases without a network round-trip. Track prices with a local watchlist that pings you on new historical lows. Pipe everything as JSON.
narrative.auth_narrative: No account, no API key, no OAuth — Blu-ray.com is read from its published HTML and XML sitemap. The CLI sends a normal browser User-Agent (configurable), throttles itself to stay under the site's per-IP budget (~4,000 pages/day), and never touches robots-disallowed paths.

quickstart.comment: Pull the public sitemap into a local SQLite + FTS5 index (~400k+ Blu-ray releases). Run weekly.
Offline title search — instant, regex-capable, no round-trip.
Fetch one release in full (specs, ratings, audio, subtitles, packaging) — cached locally.
Live 4K UHD deals at 30%+ off, narrowed to the columns an agent cares about.

quickstart.command: blu-ray-pp-cli watch add 406956 --target-price 14.99 && blu-ray-pp-cli watch check
quickstart.comment: Add a release to the local watchlist, then rescan deals and alert when the target is hit.

troubleshoots.fix: You hit Blu-ray.com's per-IP throttle (~4,000 pages/day). Wait 24h, lower concurrency (`--wait 3`), or set BLURAY_PP_PROXY.
Pages are ISO-8859-1; the parser handles this automatically. If you see mojibake, ensure your terminal is UTF-8 (`echo $LANG` should include UTF-8) — the CLI re-encodes before printing.
Run `blu-ray-pp-cli sync` once — the offline index is empty until you've synced the sitemap. After sync, `\bdoctor\b` reports the local catalog size.
The numeric `id` is stable. `releases get <stale-slug> <id>` follows the 301 to the current slug; or look up the current slug with `search` first.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for blu-ray-pp-cli whenever an agent or user needs to query the Blu-ray.com disc database: resolving titles to release ids, comparing editions across formats, scanning deals across retailers, tracking prices on a wishlist, or building a catalog drift report. The CLI is read-only against Blu-ray.com — it never logs in, never posts, and respects every robots-disallowed path. Pair it with cron or launchd to get daily price alerts without a third-party service.

recipes.title: Find every 4K UHD release of Fight Club
recipes.explanation: Offline FTS5 lookup narrowed to 4K — returns each id you can then `releases get` for full specs.

recipes.title: Daily 4K UHD preorder digest
recipes.command: blu-ray-pp-cli releases new --show comingsoon --format 4k --json --select title,release_date,distributor | jq '.[:20]'
recipes.explanation: Pipe to jq for the top 20 — drop into cron for a daily digest, no Discord webhook needed.

recipes.title: Spot a real deal vs. a fake one
recipes.command: blu-ray-pp-cli deals --country USA --format 4k --json --select release_id,title,sale_price,percent_off | jq '.[] | select(.percent_off>40)'
recipes.explanation: Filters deals to >40% off, then cross-reference each `release_id` with `blu-ray-pp-cli history <id> --plot` to confirm it's a real historical low.

recipes.title: Import a Blu-ray.com UPC export and enrich it
recipes.explanation: Round-trips Blu-ray.com's UPC-only export back into structured release data — titles, formats, ratings, current prices.

recipes.title: What dropped this week?
recipes.explanation: Counts new Blu-ray releases added to the catalog in the past 7 days. Replace `.added` with `.removed` or `.changed` for the other slices.
